title: Reversal
description: Analyze a stripped ELF binary that validates a password. Reverse the check logic and recover the correct password to get the flag.
longDescription:
A compiled Linux ELF binary prompts for a password. If correct, it prints the flag. If wrong, it exits silently. The binary is stripped — no debug symbols.

Use Ghidra or radare2 to decompile the main function, understand the password validation logic, and derive or brute-force the correct input.

### What you will practice
Static analysis in Ghidra, disassembly reading, understanding string operations in x86, and writing a keygen script.
creator: Dock & Root Labs
objectives: Load the binary in Ghidra and navigate to main()
Derive the correct password from the decompiled code
Run the binary with the correct password to reveal the flag
